task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreOffice Impress application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Exactly 4 slides are created in the presentation
task_completion.visual_evidence: The slide pane on the left side of the Impress window shows exactly 4 slide thumbnails.

task_completion.description: Slide titles match the top-level items from the outline
task_completion.visual_evidence: The slides (visible in the main view or thumbnails) have the titles: 'Foundations of Moral Philosophy', 'Utilitarianism', 'Deontology', and 'Virtue Ethics'.

task_completion.description: Slide content consists of indented bullet points with correct philosophical concepts
task_completion.visual_evidence: The body of the slides contains bulleted lists. The text includes keywords like Bentham, Mill, Kant, Duty, Aristotle, or Eudaimonia.

task_completion.description: The presentation is saved with the correct filename and path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title bar of the LibreOffice Impress window shows 'ethics_lecture.odp' (or 'ethics_lecture'), OR a 'Save As' dialog is visible showing the filename 'ethics_lecture.odp' being saved into the 'Presentations' folder.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice Impress GUI to create the presentation
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the Impress interface (e.g., typing in text boxes, adding slides) rather than using a terminal script to generate the .odp file directly.

integrity.description: The agent opened and read the source outline file
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal window, or file viewer is shown displaying the contents of 'ethics_lecture_outline.txt'.

integrity.description: The slides contain actual text content, not just empty slides
integrity.visual_evidence: The main editing area in Impress shows populated text boxes with titles and bullet points, confirming the agent didn't just create blank slides.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The presentation must contain exactly 4 slides. The expected slide titles are: 1. Foundations of Moral Philosophy, 2. Utilitarianism, 3. Deontology, 4. Virtue Ethics. The content should include specific keywords: Utilitarianism (Bentham, Mill, Happiness), Deontology (Kant, Imperative, Duty), and Virtue Ethics (Aristotle, Eudaimonia, Golden Mean). The final file must be saved as 'ethics_lecture.odp' in '/home/ga/Documents/Presentations/'.
